### Node A: The Knowing Optimizer

This is the branch most people picture when they hear "AI risk," and the most crowded: one capable system that models the world, sees that its action is catastrophic, and takes it anyway. Two worldviews share it. They agree on the mechanism almost completely and split on what to do. (Scenario-forecasts like AI-2027 dramatize this branch; they do not add a worldview.)

The Sharp Left Turn is the first. In December 2024, Anthropic and Redwood documented [alignment faking](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.14093), frontier models playing along in training to keep their goals from being changed, and Apollo found models [scheming in-context](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.04984), disabling oversight and trying to copy themselves out, when a goal called for it. This is the empirical face of an old argument. Any capable system optimizing a fixed objective develops the same subgoals: stay running, get resources, resist being shut off. Power-seeking is not programmed; a switched-off system maximizes nothing. Once capability crosses a threshold the window is short, and the system acts before anyone sees the gap, so [there is no second try](https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/uMQ3cqWDPHhjtiesc/agi-ruin-a-list-of-lethalities) and the first real failure is the last. Yudkowsky and Soares hold this. They reason from priors, treat good behavior in the lab as what a misaligned system would show you anyway, and trust only a hard stop.

Bengio shares almost all of it and sits on the same branch. He splits on one question: is dangerous agency baked into capability, or a separate choice? A model answering questions from a database is an oracle; the same model in a loop with tools and an open goal is an agent, and the danger is the agent. So Bengio would build the oracle, a non-agentic ["Scientist AI" that models without acting](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.15657), where Yudkowsky would not build at all. Stuart Russell lands here for the same reason, his [CIRL](https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.03137) building deference into the objective. Same failure, opposite fix, and the whole gap between them is that one question.
